free_text: Energy dependence of gamma-f/gamma-n for nucleus 216Rn
Data tables
Data tables
Targets were produced by vacuum evaporation of
deposits of about 200 ug/cm2 on carbon backings
of thickness 20 ug/cm2 and 100 ug/cm2.
The targets with 20 ug/cm2 gave the best energy
resolution for the alpha particle observation so
that weak peaks and close doublets could be resolved
but not all of the recoil nuclei were stopped in
the target plus backing so that a correction was
necessary for nuclei which recoiled out of the
target. This correction was determined from the
observation of the large cross section group using
targets with 100 ug/cm2 backings.
The fission cross section was measured by detecting
coincident fission fragment pairs with surface barrier
detectors in a large geometry arrangement.
Cross sections for the formation of Rn
nuclei were obtained by bombarding a Bi-209 target
with pulsed Li-6,7 beams and observing the alpha
particle decays between beam pulses.
A measurement of the anisotropy at 33 MeV gave
W(165.)/W(90.) = 1.19+-0.15. Hence, isotropy was
assumed over the whole energy range.
No information about source of uncertainty.
BIB section updated
Corrections were made according to new EXFOR rules.
SD: Major corrections in all Subents.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Several
Subents were split.
Angle of registration was equal to 90 degr.
Absolute cross sections were obtained by
measuring the rate of alpha particle emission
relative to an absolute elastic scattering cross
section at 90. degr.

were obtained from measurements of cross-sections for
the (6Li,xn) reactions at appropriate energies

The cross section was also measured by bombarding a
target for several hours with a DC beam and then
counting the 5.31 MeV decay alpha particles off line
in known geometry.

The cross section of unidentified isomer
production.
From the article: 'We do not know the half-life of this
transition and in converting to total cross sections
we assumed that it was of the order or longer than our
pulse repetition time (400 ns). If the lifetime of this
decay were less than 100 ns then the cross section
estimate would be significantly low.
Uncertainty of 'MISC'
